ai_research.headline: Tesla Q1 beats on all metrics — $22.4B revenue, 21.1% margin, FCF up 117% — Google Cloud Next drops Ironwood + Trillium TPUs

items.title: Tesla Q1 2026: BEAT across the board
items.body: Revenue $22.387B (+16% YoY) — beat consensus. Non-GAAP EPS $0.41 — beat $0.36-0.37 expectations. GAAP gross margin 21.1% (+478 bps YoY). Free cash flow $1.444B (+117% YoY). Automotive $16.2B (+16%), Services $3.7B (+42%), Energy $2.4B (-12%). Net income $477M (+17%). The margin expansion story is real — services and software are carrying. Energy dipped but 8.8 GWh deployed in Q1.

items.title: Google Cloud Next: Ironwood (training) + Trillium (inference) TPUs unveiled
items.body: Google launched two new TPU families at Cloud Next. Ironwood targets large-scale training; Trillium focuses on inference optimization. Designed for the 'agentic era' — AI agents that act autonomously. Also showing Gemini running on air-gapped servers for on-prem deployment. Directly challenges NVIDIA's dominance in both training and inference markets.

items.title: Microsoft commits $18B to AI in Australia — largest-ever investment there
items.body: Satya Nadella announced $18B for cloud and AI infrastructure expansion in Australia. Signals that AI infrastructure buildout is going global — not just US/China. Comes as Nokia shares hit 16-year high on AI data center demand.

ai_research.learn_today: Tesla's Q1 earnings are the vindication story. The market was skeptical after weak delivery numbers (358K units), but the margin expansion (+478 bps) and FCF surge (+117%) show that Tesla is becoming a software/services business, not just a car company. Services revenue up 42% is the number that matters — that's FSD subscriptions, Supercharger revenue, and insurance. The car is the platform; the margins come from everything else.

openclaw_agents.headline: Google Cloud 'agentic era' framing + OpenAI Workspace Agents — agent infrastructure week

items.title: Google Cloud Ironwood/Trillium built for 'agentic era' — agents are the use case
items.body: Google explicitly positioned its new TPUs for autonomous AI agents, not just chatbots. The inference-optimized Trillium is designed for agents that need real-time, continuous decision-making. When Google names its hardware strategy after agents, you know the market has shifted.
items.try_this: If you're building agent systems, the inference cost story is what matters. Trillium-type hardware will bring per-query costs down dramatically over the next 12 months.

items.title: OpenAI Workspace Agents: custom GPTs evolved into enterprise workflow agents
items.body: OpenAI launched Workspace Agents — successor to custom GPTs. Integrates with Slack, Salesforce, and enterprise tools for autonomous workflows. The shift from 'custom chatbot' to 'autonomous workflow agent' is the key evolution. Agents that work inside your existing tools, not alongside them.
items.try_this: Enterprise agents that integrate with existing workflows (Slack, Salesforce, etc.) will win over standalone agent chatbots. If you're building agent tools, think 'embedded' not 'destination.'

openclaw_agents.try_today: Tesla earnings were a beat — stock should react positively today. Watch the pre-market. Also: Google Cloud Next is happening this week, expect more AI announcements daily.

tesla.note: Q1 earnings BEAT after close yesterday: $22.4B rev (+16% YoY), $0.41 non-GAAP EPS (beat $0.36 consensus), 21.1% gross margin (+478 bps), $1.4B FCF (+117%). Services revenue up 42% is the standout. Energy down 12% but 8.8 GWh deployed. Pre-market reaction to watch today — was at $386 close, consensus expected lower.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: SpaceX-Cursor deal from yesterday still dominating headlines. Also: reports SpaceX eyeing in-house GPUs amid chip shortages, possibly for orbital AI data centers. IPO timeline still confidential. GPS III-8 satellite maneuvering to operational orbit.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Cursor deal benefits xAI through shared Colossus infrastructure. No standalone xAI news. Grok integration into Tesla vehicles via spring update continues rolling out.

items.title: This Is a Gardening Show
items.note: Dropped today. Zach Galifianakis hosting a whimsical gardening series. If you like weird comfort TV with funny hosts, this is your Wednesday night pick.

items.title: Lainey Wilson: Keepin' Country Cool
items.note: Dropped today. Country music documentary. Worth it if you're a fan; skippable if you're not.

items.title: Million Dollar Secret — Season 2
items.note: Dropped today. Murder mystery dinner party reality show. Easy binge if you like that genre.

items.title: Criminal Record — Season 2
items.note: Dropped yesterday. Peter Capaldi and Cush Jumbo return. If you liked Season 1's London crime thriller, this is a must-watch.

items.title: Tesla earnings beat is the positioning proof point for any autonomy/AI narrative
items.body: Tesla's margin expansion story (21.1% gross margin, +478 bps) validates the 'cars are platforms' thesis. Services revenue up 42% shows software/FSD is carrying. If InstaClaw has any autonomy or platform positioning, 'Tesla proved it Q1' is the data point to cite.

items.title: OpenAI Workspace Agents — if QuizzyDots ever goes B2B, this is the product category
items.body: OpenAI launching agents embedded in Slack/Salesforce is the category definition moment. 'Agents that live in your tools' vs 'agents you go to.' If QuizzyDots ever has a workplace/enterprise play, embedding in existing tools is the winning distribution model.

macro.mood: Markets slightly green. TSLA should pop on earnings beat — watch pre-market. Google Cloud Next driving AI infrastructure narrative. Nokia hit 16-year high on AI demand. BTC pulled back to $77.5K after touching $78.3K yesterday. Microsoft $18B Australia investment shows global AI capex is just getting started.
